name: NEW_TARGETPAIRS_FOR_TOP_ASSIGNEES
description: New target pairs for top assignees. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (ymax:Year)
WITH max(ymax.year) AS max_year
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p0:Patent)-[:PUBLISHED_IN]->(y0:Year)
WITH tp, min(y0.year) AS first_year, max_year
WHERE first_year >= max_year - $years + 1
MATCH (tp)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p:Patent)-[:HAS_ASSIGNEE]->(a:Assignee)
WITH a, tp, count(DISTINCT p) AS patent_count
ORDER BY patent_count DESC, tp.name
WITH a, collect(DISTINCT tp.name)[0..10] AS target_pairs, count(DISTINCT tp) AS emerging_targetpair_count
RETURN a.name AS assignee, emerging_targetpair_count, target_pairs
ORDER BY emerging_targetpair_count DESC, assignee
LIMIT 10

examples: For companies that were highly active in novel target-pair filings over the last three years, which specific combinations were they associated with?
Which novel target-pair combinations are linked to the companies leading recent filings in the last three years?
Among the companies filing many new combinations in the last three years, what target-pair combinations did they actually cover?
